metadata.title:
NO PRECEDENT
ON EARTH

w. W. p. co. PAID TAXES ON is
MILES OF RAILROAD THAT
EXISTED ONLY IN GENERAL
MANAGERS IMAGINATION.

pages.raw_text:
The valuation of the Washington


Water Power streetcar lines will be


just 16 miles short this year OF


what it was last, and the county shy


that much taxes. The company has
not torn up that much track MOI
has 1t been able to induce the as
SCISSOR to overlook it.


It's on General Manager Hunting
ton, who is affidavit man" when il
comes to enumerating the w. w


P. taxable property. Last year


land this Is 1 Of the weird and
wondrous incident In corporation


history, Mr.. Huntington made oath
that the company owned 16 more


miles of line than it had laid. He
signified willingness to pay taxes


on that stretch Of imaginary rail


road, and naturally the county col


lected.


The mistake was not discovered


until after the county board OF


equalization had completed its


work and then it was too late fol


even the w. w. P. to do anything
And the county was ahead a few


hundred, maybe, on the deal. OF
course the company might have


made it up some way, might have
held it out of Manager Huntington
salary, but that part of the story is
mystery.


This is mighty hard to believe
but it is claimed to be a matter OF
record at the courthouse.
